opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The moment my foot hits the floor in the morning, a blinding, stabbing pain shoots straight through my heel. It's so bad this morning I could barely stand up and get across the room.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The heel pain has been niggling for a couple of weeks but the last two days it has been excruciating on the first steps in the morning and I can hardly put weight on it.

symptom_narrative.onset: mild for about 2 weeks, then much worse over the last 48 hours after I switched to some flat, unsupportive shoes
symptom_narrative.duration: 2 weeks, sharply worse in the last 2 days
symptom_narrative.character: sharp, stabbing, like stepping on a nail, right under the heel
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the very first steps in the morning are the worst pain, almost unbearable
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: right on the bottom of my heel, more towards the inside edge
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: worst with the first steps in the morning, eases a bit once I get moving, then gets bad again after I've been standing or on my feet for a while
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: it settles a little after I've walked around for a few minutes; resting off my feet helps
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: walking barefoot on hard floors like the kitchen tiles, and standing for a long time
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: none — no pins and needles, no numbness, and it doesn't shoot up my leg
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm limping and walking on the front of my foot to keep weight off the heel; hard to get around the house

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: paracetamol — I took 1000mg but it barely touched the pain

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; getting around the house is difficult at the moment because of the pain on standing; floors at home are hard wood and tile throughout

objective_data_offered.value: the bottom of my heel and foot look normal to me — no swelling, no redness, no bruising
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient's own visual impression on video, not a clinical inspection

objective_data_offered.value: I'm walking on my toes and the front of my foot, limping, trying not to put my heel down
